editAgentRounds.reply:
Got it — you think your subscription expired; I’ll help clarify and walk through next steps so we can get you back up (or confirm everything’s fine).

Checklist
- [ ] Confirm which subscription/service you mean (OpenAI/ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, or something else)
- [ ] Show how to check subscription/billing status and common fixes
- [ ] Offer hands‑on help or next steps (renew, update payment, contact support, or continue working here)

Quick clarifying question
- Which subscription did you mean — your ChatGPT/OpenAI plan, GitHub Copilot, something from your IDE (Eclipse plugin), or another service?

If you’re not sure, try these quick checks
- Check your email for a renewal or expiration message from the service (search for “receipt”, “renewal”, or the service name).
- Sign in to the service’s website and look for Account → Billing or Subscriptions. That page shows active/expired status and last payment method.
- Check your payment method (bank/credit card / PayPal) for a recent charge or a failed payment notification.
- If a payment failed, update the payment method and retry the purchase/renewal from the billing page.

If you want to contact support, include
- Your account email, the approximate date of the last successful charge (if any), and the last 4 digits of the card used (don’t paste full card numbers). Short subject: “Billing/subscription question — account [your email]”.

I can help next by
- Walking you step-by-step through the billing page if you tell me which service it is.
- Drafting a support message to send.
- Or, if you were worried I’d stop helping here, don’t worry — I can continue assisting with your project right now. Tell me what you want me to do next (run build, fix tests, modify files, etc.).

Which subscription should I look into for you?

editAgentRounds.reply: I'll create a short actionable checklist for restoring/checking your GitHub Copilot subscription, then give step-by-step instructions and a draft support message you can use — I'll mark the first task in-progress so we stay organized.

Thanks — good to know it’s GitHub Copilot. Here’s how I’ll proceed next and what I need from you.

Plan: confirm your GitHub account and environment, then walk through checking billing and the Copilot plugin, and finally provide steps to reactivate or draft a support message if needed.

Checklist
- [in-progress] Confirm account & environment — I’ll need your GitHub account email, whether Copilot is personal or org-managed, and whether you use Copilot in Eclipse (and any error messages you see).
- [ ] Check GitHub billing page
- [ ] Check Copilot status in Eclipse
- [ ] Update payment / Reactivate
- [ ] Contact GitHub Support (draft)

Quick questions (reply with answers or say “I don’t know”)
1) Which GitHub account/email is signed into the Copilot plugin (or the account you think the subscription is under)?
2) Is Copilot for personal use (your account) or provided through an organization (company/school)?
3) Are you using Copilot in Eclipse? If so, which Eclipse version? Do you see any Copilot error messages in the IDE (e.g., “Sign in to GitHub”, “Subscription expired”, or plugin errors)? If yes, paste the exact message or a screenshot description.
4) Do you have access to the billing/owner account if this is an org subscription? (Yes/No)

If you prefer, I can give step-by-step instructions to check billing and plugin status without sharing account details — tell me which you'd like.

todos.title: Confirm account & environment
todos.description: Ask which GitHub account/email and whether Copilot is used personally or through an organization; confirm IDE (Eclipse) and whether you see any Copilot error messages in the IDE.

todos.title: Check GitHub billing page
todos.description: Sign in at github.com, go to Settings → Billing or Organizations → Settings → Billing (if using org), and verify Copilot subscription status and invoices.

todos.title: Check Copilot status in Eclipse
todos.description: Open Eclipse Installation Details/Preferences to find Copilot plugin and check sign-in status or error logs; try to sign in from the plugin if necessary.

todos.title: Update payment / Reactivate
todos.description: If subscription expired, update payment method and reactivate Copilot from the GitHub billing/subscription UI (or ask org owner to add a seat).

todos.title: Contact GitHub Support (draft)
todos.description: If billing issues persist, send a support message with account email, last charge date, subscription ID, and screenshots. Provide a ready-to-send draft message.
